title: At An Inn In England (Epigrams On Windows)
content:
The glass, by lovers' nonsense blurr'd,
        Dims and obscures our sight;
    So, when our passions Love has stirr'd,
        It darkens Reason's light.
meaning:
Jonathan Swift's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "At An Inn In England (Epigrams On Windows)"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
